exercises.title: PULSE COUNTER
exercises.brief: A private tally that only goes up.
promptLines: Define a class **Counter** with a **private** number field that **starts at 0**, a method **tick(): void** that adds 1, and a method **value(): number** returning the current count.
starterLines: // TODO: private count field starting at 0

tests.name: two ticks
instances are independent

exercises.hint: private count: number = 0; then this.count += 1 in tick and return this.count in value.

exercises.title: TYPED CRATE
exercises.brief: Seal any payload in a typed crate.
promptLines: Define a generic class **Box<T>** using constructor shorthand `constructor(private value: T)` and a method **get(): T** returning the stored value.
starterLines: get(): T { return undefined as any; }
exercises.hint: constructor(private value: T) {} then return this.value;

exercises.title: ZONE CHECK
exercises.brief: Is the target inside the perimeter?
promptLines: Define a class **Range** using constructor shorthand `constructor(private lo: number, private hi: number)` and a method **contains(x: number): boolean** that is `true` when `x` is between `lo` and `hi` **inclusive**.
starterLines: constructor(private lo: number, private hi: number) {}
solutionLines: constructor(private lo: number, private hi: number) {}
contains(x: number): boolean { return x >= this.lo && x <= this.hi; }

tests.name: outside
tests.code: assert(new Range(1, 10).contains(11) === false && new Range(1, 10).contains(0) === false);

exercises.lore: Cross the line and the turrets wake.

exercises.title: CRED WALLET
promptLines: Define a class **Wallet** with a **private** balance. The constructor takes a starting amount (default 0). Add **deposit(n: number): void**, **withdraw(n: number): boolean** (return `false` and change nothing if `n` exceeds the balance, else subtract and return `true`), and **balance(): number**.

tests.name: deposit raises balance
overdraft refused
tests.code:
var w = new Wallet(100);
assert(w.withdraw(200) === false && w.balance() === 100, 'no overdraft, balance unchanged');

tests.name: valid withdrawal
tests.code:
var w = new Wallet(100);
assert(w.withdraw(60) === true && w.balance() === 40);

exercises.hint: Store this.bal; withdraw checks n > this.bal and returns false before changing anything.
exercises.lore: The street ledger never extends credit.

exercises.title: RUNNING SUM
exercises.brief: Fold a stream of numbers into one total.
promptLines: Define a class **Accumulator** with a **private** total that **starts at 0**. Add **add(n: number): number** that adds `n` to the total and **returns the new running total**, and **total(): number** returning the current total.
starterLines: // TODO: private sum field starting at 0
solutionLines: add(n: number): number { this.sum += n; return this.sum; }

tests.name: add returns running total
tests.code:
var acc = new Accumulator();
assert(acc.add(3) === 3 && acc.add(4) === 7, 'add returns the updated total');

tests.name: total reflects the sum
instances are independent

exercises.hint: private sum = 0; in add do this.sum += n then return this.sum.
exercises.lore: Drip by drip, the meter climbs.
